2604.12537.title: MODIX: A Training-Free Multimodal Information-Driven Positional Index Scaling for Vision-Language Models
2604.12537.paper_summary: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have achieved remarkable progress in multimodal understanding, yet their positional encoding mechanisms remain suboptimal. Existing approaches uniformly assign positional indices to all tokens, overlooking variations in information density within and across modalities, which leads to inefficient attention allocation where redundant visual regions dominate while informative content is underrepresented. We identify positional granularity as an implicit resource and propose MODIX (Multimodal Information-Driven Positional IndeX Scaling), a training-free framework that dynamically adapts positional strides based on modality-specific contributions. MODIX jointly models intra-modal density via covariance-based entropy and inter-modal interaction via cross-modal alignment to derive unified scores, which rescale positional indices to allocate finer granularity to informative modalities while compressing redundant ones, without requiring any modification to model parameters or architecture. Experiments across diverse architectures and benchmarks demonstrate that MODIX consistently improves multimodal reasoning and adaptively reallocates attention according to task-dependent information distributions, suggesting that positional encoding should be treated as an adaptive resource in Transformers for multimodal sequence modeling.
2604.12537.paper_summary_zh: 視覺-語言模型（VLMs）在多模態理解方面取得了顯著進展，但其位置編碼機制仍然不夠理想。現有的方法對所有標記均勻分配位置索引，忽略了模態內部和跨模態的信息密度變化，導致注意力分配效率低下，冗餘的視覺區域主導，而有用的內容則被低估。我們將位置粒度識別為一種隱性資源，並提出MODIX（多模態信息驅動的位置索引縮放），這是一個無需訓練的框架，根據模態特定的貢獻動態調整位置步幅。MODIX通過基於協方差的熵建模模態內密度，並通過跨模態對齊建模模態間互動，以推導統一分數，這些分數重新調整位置索引，以便將更細的粒度分配給有用的模態，同時壓縮冗餘的模態，而無需對模型參數或架構進行任何修改。在多種架構和基準上的實驗表明，MODIX持續改善多模態推理，並根據任務依賴的信息分佈自適應地重新分配注意力，這表明位置編碼應被視為Transformer在多模態序列建模中的一種自適應資源。
2604.12537.author: Ruoxiang Huang et.al.
